pattern: Fixing the left edge of a subarray and sliding the right edge forward lets you maintain a running distinct-value count with a frequency map instead of recomputing it from scratch for every subarray.
hints: There are only n*(n+1)/2 subarrays for an array of length n, small enough to enumerate directly by fixing the start index.
For a fixed start index, grow the end index one step at a time and keep a frequency map; the distinct count only changes when a value's frequency goes from zero to one.
Accumulate the square of the running distinct count after each extension of the window, then move to the next start index and reset the frequency map.

edge_inputs.description: one repeated value in the middle
all distinct values, every subarray's distinct count equals its length
all identical values, every subarray has distinct count one
longer mixed pattern with partial repeats

stress.description: random array at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
